item.title: The Imagine Dragons Brothers Try to Take on the Video-Game Market
item.description: With ‘Last Flag,’ lifelong gamers Dan and Mac Reynolds are trying to enter an overcrowded market

item.title: Fed’s Waller Signals Caution On Rate Cuts, Sees Risk of Longer Conflict
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said he is cautious about the need to lower interest rates in the near term, due to the energy shock triggered by war in Iran, and warned of the risk of a prolonged impact on inflation due to the conflict.

item.title: Brazil Treasury Sees Room for More Foreign-Exchange-Linked Debt
item.description: Brazil’s government sees room to grow its share of foreign-exchange-linked debt even further following this year’s planned expansion, Treasury Secretary Daniel Leal said in an interview.

item.title: Anthropic’s Amodei to Meet Wiles With US Seeking Mythos Access
item.description: Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei is scheduled to meet on Friday with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Trump administration seeks wider US government access to the company’s powerful new Mythos artificial intelligence model.

item.title: Obesity Firm Kailera Therapeutics Up 63% After $625 Million IPO
item.description: Kailera Therapeutics Inc. shares surged 63% after the clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on obesity raised $625 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

item.title: Oil Tankers Make Dash Toward Hormuz as Iran Says Strait Is Open
item.description: At least eight oil tankers raced toward the Strait of Hormuz in the hours after Iran’s foreign minister said the vital waterway was fully open to shipping.

item.title: Hedge Funds’ Treasury Bets Risk Market Shock, Apollo’s Slok Warns
item.description: A buildup of leveraged hedge fund bets in Treasuries has left investors exposed to abrupt position shifts that could amplify stress across global bond markets, according to Apollo Global Management Chief Economist Torsten Slok.

item.title: Short Seller Targets AQR Backer Over Tax-Loss Harvesting
item.description: A short seller is taking aim at the more than $1 trillion that’s invested in strategies that reduce taxes for the rich — putting one of the US’ most staid money managers in its crosshairs.

item.title: Costa Rica Says China Failed to Fulfill Trade Deal Expectations
item.description: Costa Rica is dissatisfied with the volume of its exports to China under its free trade deal with the Asian giant, according to a minister from the Central American nation.

item.title: Alcoa Is Close to a Deal to Sell Smelter Site to Bitcoin Miner
item.description: Alcoa Corp. is in advanced talks to sell a former smelter site in upstate New York to Bitcoin mining firm NYDIG as part of its plans to dispose of its dormant assets.

item.title: Medical Device Maker Alamar Bio Jumps 33% After Upsized US IPO
item.description: Alamar Biosciences Inc. shares climbed 33% on Friday after the medical device maker raised $191 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of a marketed range.

item.title: North Sea Oil Plunges After Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Reopened
item.description: North Sea crude oil prices plunged in a key pricing window after Iran said it would reopen the Strait of Hormuz during a ceasefire in the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Warsh Hearing Will Test How Far He’ll Push Balance Sheet Reform
item.description: Kevin Warsh will get his first major public platform as Federal Reserve Chair nominee next week to address his longstanding concerns over the central bank’s outsized market footprint.

item.title: Shipowners and Oil Traders Wary as Iran Says Hormuz Fully Open
item.description: Tanker owners and oil traders responded warily after Iran said on Friday that the Strait of Hormuz is open to all ships — a statement that, if true, will free up millions of barrels of crude oil and fuel stuck inside the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Drone Maker Aevex Shares Climb 15% After $320 Million US IPO
item.description: Aevex Corp. shares rose 15% after the maker of military drones raised $320 million in a US initial public offering.

item.title: Software’s Rebound Puts Stocks On Track for Best Week Since 2001
item.description: An exchanged-traded fund trading software stocks is climbing again Friday, putting it on track for the best week in 25 years.

item.title: Air Canada Suspends Summer Flights to JFK Over Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Air Canada will suspend daily flights to John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York City amid soaring jet fuel costs.

item.title: IMF Sees Canada’s Fiscal Position as Strongest in Group of Seven
item.description: The International Monetary Fund lauded Canada’s fiscal position relative to its peers as Prime Minister Mark Carney prepares to give an update on his government’s finances.

item.title: US Mineral Stockpile to Be Open to All Traders, Ex-Im Bank Says
item.description: A $12 billion critical minerals stockpile project being developed by the US Export-Import Bank will be open to all trading companies, rather than just those named as suppliers when the initiative was launched in February.

item.title: Roark Said to Pick Banks for Dunkin’ Owner’s $2 Billion US IPO
item.description: Roark Capital has picked banks to work on a US initial public offering of Inspire Brands, the owner of fast-food chains Dunkin’, Arby’s and Jimmy John’s, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: The Private Credit Industry Is Growing Up. That’s Rarely a Painless Process
item.description: Also: Who’s actually buying Cybertrucks?

item.title: Emerging Currencies Erase War Losses as Traders Bet on Peace
item.description: A key gauge of emerging-market currencies has fully recovered its losses from the start of the US-Iran war, driven by optimism that the conflict is de-escalating after Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US Eyes More Namibian Uranium Imports as AI Drives Nuclear Push
item.description: The US is weighing boosting uranium imports from Namibia, the world’s third-biggest producer, and supporting greater output of the nuclear fuel through its mining companies and government financing, America’s top diplomat in the southern African nation said.

item.title: Canada Stocks Recover Losses From Iran Conflict as Strait Opens
item.description: Canadian stocks jumped Friday and reversed all of their losses since the war in the Middle East began after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is open to commercial ships, fueling hopes the conflict may be nearing an end.

item.title: Bitcoin Climbs to Two-Month High Amid Middle East Deal Optimism
item.description: Bitcoin climbed to the highest level since early February after a flurry of comments from the US and Iran sparked optimism that the conflict in the Middle East may be heading toward a resolution.

item.title: Venezuela Bonds Rally as IMF Resumes Formal Contact with Caracas
item.description: Venezuela’s dollar bonds are rallying on Friday as the International Monetary Fund’s decision to resume contact with authorities in Caracas lifts investor sentiment toward the nation’s defaulted debt.

item.title: Apple Upgraded as BNP Paribas Sees Path to Increase Market Share
item.description: Apple Inc. was upgraded to outperform from neutral by BNP Paribas, which said the iPhone maker can use the recent spike in memory prices as a way to increase its share of smartphone market share.

item.title: Iran Conflict Poses Risk to Global Economy, IMF Members to Say
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is a major shock that poses a “serious threat to the global economy” and will hit the poorest countries the hardest, according to a statement expected to be released by the Chair of the International Monetary and Financial Committee Friday.

item.title: Russia’s Main Black Sea Port Resumes Loading Crude at Key Berth
item.description: Russia’s largest Black Sea port of Novorossiysk resumed loading crude from a key berth, according to people familiar with the information.

item.title: Treasuries Rally as Oil Tumbles on Mideast Peace Deal Optimism
item.description: Treasuries jumped, sending yields to their lowest levels in a month, as oil prices tumbled on optimism a Middle East peace deal is within reach.
